question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user submit a TopicMessage with payerAccountId referencing a smart contract account rather than a regular account, causing the fee assessment logic to apply contract-execution fee rules instead of topic-submission fee rules, modifying the effective fee outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the @Transactional annotation on RetentionRepository.prune() to cause a transaction rollback after partial deletion of TopicMessage records, leaving the database in a state where some fee records are deleted and others are not, creating an inconsistent fee audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user submit a TopicMessage with chunkTotal set to a value much larger than the actual number of chunks submitted, causing the importer to hold partial chunk state in memory and defer fee finalization indefinitely, effectively deferring fee payment beyond the designed settlement window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/repository/TopicMessageRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: prune(long consensusTimestamp)] Can an unprivileged user submit a TopicMessage with validStartTimestamp equal to consensusTimestamp, causing the fee calculation to treat the message as having zero valid duration and applying a different fee tier than designed for normal-duration messages?
